original: The US team ofMichael Phelps,Aaron Peirsol, Brendan Hansen, and Jason Lezak wins the 4 x 100 m medley relay at the Beijing Olympics in a world record time of 3:29.34; Phelps's 8th gold medal of the Games breaks Mark Spitz's 1972 record of 7
Thomas Wessinghage, Harald Hudak, Michael Lederer, and Karl Fleschen set the world record in the 4 x 1500 m relay with a time of 14:38.8
Jamaican sprinter Elaine Thompson wins the 200 m gold medal in 21.78, wrapping up the 100/200 m Rio de Janeiro Olympics double
Collision of two neutron stars is witnessed for the first time, first detected by the US-based Laser Interferometer Gravitational-Wave Observatory (LIGO)
Bodhana Sivanandan (10) defeats grandmaster Pete Wells (60) in final round of 2025 British Chess Championships in Liverpool, England, becoming youngest female player to beat a grandmaster
Musical film "The Wizard of Oz," starringJudy Garland, Ray Bolger, Bert Lahr, Jack Haley, and Margaret Hamilton, opens at Loew's Capitol Theatre, NYC
Russian nuclear-powered icebreaker "Arktika" is the first surface ship to reach the North Pole
American astronomer Asaph Hall discovers Mars's moon Phobos
Phillies get 36 hits as Sam Thompson hits for the cycle, beating Louisville 29-4
First successful crossing of the Atlantic by balloon is completed by Americans Ben Abruzzo, Maxie Anderson, and Larry Newman aboard Double Eagle II

Australian women's 4 x 100 m medley relay team of Emily Seebohm, Leisel Jones, Jessicah Schipper, and Libby Trickett upsets the United States to win the gold medal in a world record time of 3:52.69 at the Beijing Olympics

China wins its 33rd gold medal of the Beijing Olympics by defeating Singapore in the table tennis women's team event, achieving its most successful Olympiad ever by winning all 10 singles and all 5 doubles matches

Four years after winning Olympic silver in the rowing quad sculls in Athens, Briton Rebecca Romero becomes only the second woman to earn medals in two different sports at the Summer Games when she wins the pursuit track cycling gold in Beijing
Possible second smaller impact crater that wiped out the dinosaurs, the "Nadir Crater," 8.5 km across, is identified off the coast of Guinea, West Africa
George Orwellpublishes "Animal Farm" in the United Kingdom
Margaret Hamilton, American computer scientist, systems engineer (MIT), and Presidential Medal of Freedom recipient (2016, for her work with NASA's Apollo missions), born in Paoli, Indiana
Soviet spacecraft Venera 7 is launched towards Venus
Eugene Kranz, American NASA Flight Director (Gemini and Apollo programs, including 1st Moon landing), born in Toledo, Ohio
Rodney Mullen, American skateboarder ("Godfather of modern freestyle skating"; 35 x world titles), born in Gainesville, Florida
Bronze pig statue, named "Rachel" after a real 750-pound pig who won the 1985 Island County Fair, is unveiled at Pike Place Market, Seattle
MLB playerLou Gehrigplays his record 1,308th consecutive game

Baseball playerRichie Ashburnfouls and hits fan Alice Roth twice in the same at-bat while playing for the Philadelphia Phillies; the first hit breaks her nose, and the second one hits her while she is on the stretcher

Columbia Records releases "Kind of Blue," a studio album by American jazz trumpeter and composerMiles Davis, leading a sextet featuring saxophonistJohn Coltraneand pianist Bill Evans; the work is widely considered a masterpiece and is one of the most influential and best-selling albums of Davis' long career
Kathryn C. Thornton, American astronaut (STS 33, 49, 61, 73), born in Montgomery, Alabama
Projection in Paris of the first animated cartoon, "Fantasmagorie," created by Émile Cohl
China issues its highest red alert heat warning for at least 138 cities and counties amid the country's longest heatwave since records began, lasting 64 days
Huge wildfires begin on Gran Canaria in the Canary Islands, prompting the evacuation of 9,000 people
Frederick F. Russell, American army physician, developed 1st successful typhoid fever vaccine, born in Auburn, New York (d. 1960)
Ralph Teetor, American automotive engineer (1st blind engineer in the US, inventor of cruise control), born in Hagerstown, Indiana (d. 1982)[1]
World's first flower could be the underwater plant Montsechia vidalii, claim US botanists (Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences)
Robert Fulton's steamboat Clermont begins its first trip up the Hudson River
Charles Darwinleaves South America for the last time on HMS Beagle
7.1-magnitude earthquake strikes Yellowstone National Park
Boycott scores his first Test cricket century: 113 against Australia at The Oval
NASA satellite Pioneer 7 launched into solar orbit
Azaria Chamberlain disappears, likely taken by a dingo, leading to one of the most publicized trials in Australian history
Hurricane Andrew forms southeast of Barbados in the Caribbean
Chinese researchers find an MD5 collision
On This Day helps launch Borneo Dictionary, an online nonprofit dictionary of indigenous Borneo languages with translations in English and Bahasa Malaysia
Laura de Force Gordon, California lawyer, a prominent suffragette, and 1st woman to run a daily newspaper in the US (Stockton Daily Leader, 1874), born in North East, Pennsylvania (d. 1907)

Floyd Red Crow Westerman, Native American (Dakota Sioux) country music singer-songwriter, actor (Walker, Texas Ranger; Northern Exposure), and political activist, born on Lake Traverse Indian Reservation, South Dakota (d. 2007)
Robert Thirsk, Canadian astronaut (STS 78), born in New Westminster, British Columbia
First airmail in a balloon takes off from Lafayette, Indiana
World's first Moon probe, USA's Thor-Able, explodes at T+77 seconds
Electric self-starter for automobiles patented
Tom Henrich hits his record-tying fourth grand slam of the season
18th NFL Chicago All-Star Game: Cleveland 33, All-Stars 0 (92,180 attendees)
Hurricane winds drive six ships ashore in Kingston, Jamaica
Hurricane Diane follows Hurricane Connie and floods the Connecticut River, killing 190 people and causing $1.8 billion in damage
First compact discs (CDs) released to the public in Germany
NY Central Park Reservoir officially named after Jackie Kennedy Onassis
Soyuz TM-24 is launched into orbit
Charles Darwinreaches the top of Campana in Chile during his voyage on the Beagle
Joseph Pulitzerdonates $1 million to Columbia University and begins the Pulitzer Prizes in the United States
Bunt single gives Milwaukee Brewers'Paul Molitorthe longest MLB hit streak of the 1980s at 39 games
Muhammad Aliis elected to "Ring" magazine's Boxing Hall of Fame
Rafael Nadalof Spain beats Chile's Fernando González 6-3, 7-6, 6-3 to win the men's tennis singles at the Beijing Olympics, Spain's first-ever Olympic gold medal in tennis
Chaleo Yoovidhya, Thai co-creator of Red Bull energy drink, born in Phichit, Thailand (d. 2012)
Jean-Jacques Sempé, French cartoonist (Le Petit Nicolas), born in Pessac, France (d. 2022)[1]
Richard of Shrewsbury, Duke of York, son of King Edward IV, one of the princes in the tower who disappeared, born in Shrewsbury, England (d. 1483?)
